foundation.title: POINTER
foundation.subtitle: Memory Index
foundation.content: A C++ pointer stores the literal memory address (like 0x7ffd) of a variable. It's the key to high-performance game data.
foundation.scenario: A Physical Pager. You have the pager device (the pointer). It points to EXACTLY one person (The data) who is in an exact house (Memory).

examples.title: Creation
examples.explanation: Asking for 4 bytes of RAM using the '*' symbol.

examples.title: Extraction
examples.explanation: Retrieving the literal memory address using the '&' (Address of) symbol.

examples.title: The Null
examples.explanation: Assigning 'nullptr' to a pointer to show it's currently pointing at nothing.

foundation.keyPoint: * = Value at. & = Address of. nullptr = Empty.

foundation.title: REFERENCE
foundation.subtitle: Aliases
foundation.content: A Reference is just a second name for an existing variable. It's easier and safer than a pointer because it can never be null.
foundation.scenario: A Nickname. You call your friend 'Robot' (Alias). If you give 'Robot' a coin, your friend has the coin. They are the same person.

examples.title: Creation
examples.explanation: Defining a reference using the '&' symbol next to the type name.

examples.title: The Alias
examples.explanation: Modifying the reference changes the original variable instantly.

examples.title: Pass Ref
examples.explanation: Using a reference as a function parameter to avoid copying large data.

foundation.keyPoint: References are fixed nicknames. They can't be null.

foundation.title: ALLOC
foundation.subtitle: Dynamic Heap
foundation.content: C++ uses 'new' and 'delete' instead of C's malloc and free. It's built to create more complex objects on the 'Heap'.
foundation.scenario: A Hotel Room. You call (new) and ask for a room for 5 people. You can keep the room as long as you want until you Check Out (delete).

examples.title: The New
examples.explanation: Asking the OS for RAM to store a single object or an array.

examples.title: The Delete
examples.explanation: Giving the memory back to the OS so it doesn't leak away.

examples.title: Array Delete
examples.explanation: A special syntax to delete an entire array of items from memory at once.

foundation.keyPoint: Always pair every 'new' with exactly one 'delete'.

gameData.q: What is the safest empty pointer?
